title: Phaca speirocarpa
intro: Phaca speciosa, also known as Phaca speirocarpa, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Fabaceae family, commonly found in the mountainous regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Phaca speirocarpa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional Chinese medicine formulations.
taxonomy: Phaca speirocarpa belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family, which comprises a wide variety of plants. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its bean-like fruit and compound leaves. Phaca speirocarpa is a genus that is typically classified as a type of flowering plant. The plant's classification is further specified to the subfamily Faboideae, which is a subfamily of the Fabaceae family. Within the subfamily, Phaca speirocarpa is further classified into its specific genus and species.
